subject: Re: AREtomo to WARP
body:
Dear Benedikt,

You can control the AreTomo behavior of excluding bad tilts (too dark) with
the -DarkTol <threshold> option. The default is -DarkTol 0.7. According to
the manual, lower values exclude fewer images so you could try something
like 0.1. But I don't know what happens if you set it to zero...

Best wishes,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em sex., 16 de jun. de 2023 às 20:28, 'B Wimmer' via Warp <
warp-em@googlegroups.com> escreveu:

> Hi Jesse, Hi all,
>
> Just to make sure I correctly understand your workflow, this is what I
> understood:
>
> - Preprocessing (Motion Correction, CTF estimation) of movies in Warp
> - Create (unbinned) stack in Warp using the imod IO dialog
> - Align this stack in AreTomo with flags -OutImod 2 and -TiltCor 0 or -1
> - Use the resulting .xf file to import alignments to Warp
>
> I am using an AreTomo - Warp pipeline in the same way and for me it seems
> to work well... The only issue is that AreTomo sometimes excludes tilts
> which it deems to dark, these would need to be excluded from the stack (in
> the preprocessing window, annoyingly) and the mdoc... Also, -OutImod 2 does
> not transfer the local alignments (generated via -Patch 4 4), but only the
> global transformation.
>
> Best,
> Benedikt
>
>
> On Thursday, June 15, 2023 at 4:20:21 PM UTC+2 lorenzo...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> > Tried reconstruction AREtomo with VolZ to 0 but it had no difference.
>> Out of curiosity, why did you make this suggestion?  Wondering how it helps
>> with troubleshooting.  Thanks!
>>
>> It will create an aligned tilt series instead of a tomogram
>> reconstruction. It should look extremely different, since it's not a
>> volume. It can help make sure the alignment transforms are sensible, as you
>> will be able to see whether the tilt series is properly aligned or it
>> "jumps around". If you see no difference (i.e: you still get a tomogram),
>> you probably passed the option wrong, or you're opening the wrong file ;)
>> I'm not sure how helpful it would be to see that the stack looks
>> bad/good... but maybe it's a starting point to find the issue :)
>>
>> > I guess if the handedness is incorrect that would be the problem, but I
>> did check handedness.
>>
>> Wrong handedness would be barely noticeable at this stage (other than the
>> tomogram being z-mirrored I think) so it's probably not the problem.
>>
>> > I rotated WARP 90 degrees CW to match orientation of the AREtomo.
>>
>> You can actually ask aretomo to give you a properly rotated tomogram
>> (-FlipVol 1)
>>
>>
>> Best,
>> Lorenzo
>>
>>
>>
>> On Thu, Jun 15, 2023 at 3:46 PM mrJ <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi All,
>>>
>>> Tried reconstruction AREtomo with VolZ to 0 but it had no difference.
>>> Out of curiosity, why did you make this suggestion?  Wondering how it helps
>>> with troubleshooting.  Thanks!
>>>
>>> Not sure what to do here.  It seems nothing is making a difference.  Is
>>> the tomogram CTF corrected which comes out of WARP?  I guess so.   Is it
>>> possible something is going wrong here?  I guess if the handedness is
>>> incorrect that would be the problem, but I did check handedness.   I feel
>>> like I've tried tinkering with all things in WARP, so I'm wondering if
>>> there's something in AREtomo to tweak.
>>>
>>> My next step is to try this with Etomo and see if importing works.  I
>>> had initially gone this route but it was not giving great results using
>>> patch tracking: jumping all over the place.  I will go try it again with
>>> drawing contours for areas to track.
>>>
>>> I've also included a screenshot of the same slice to show how different
>>> the results are.  I rotated WARP 90 degrees CW to match orientation of the
>>> AREtomo.
>>>
>>> thanks again
>>>
>>> Jesse
>>>
>>>
>>> [image: Screenshot 2023-06-15 at 3.45.17 PM.png]
>>>
>>> On Thursday, June 15, 2023 at 1:51:23 PM UTC+2 mrJ wrote:
>>>
>>>> Thank you.  I tried the pixel size change and it had no effect.  See
>>>> steps below.
>>>>
>>>>    - upon importing stack from imod, in the popup window I put pixel
>>>>    size used in imod:   2.183 (this is unbinned, 1/8th of the 17.464 I had
>>>>    previously).
>>>>    - Then in the main WARP window in upper left for pixel X/Y I also
>>>>    have 2.183.   For bin I have 0x. (2.183).   For unbinned tomo dimensions I
>>>>    have ~3712 x 3712 x 1640.  These are unbinned units.
>>>>    - Then when I reconstruct tomogram I put 17.464 again, because I
>>>>    want my tomo binned 8X.
>>>>
>>>> I am currently trying to process in AREtomo with VolZ set to 0, then
>>>> will try again.   Will report back soon.
>>>>
>>>> thanks again everyone.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Thursday, June 15, 2023 at 1:13:07 PM UTC+2 lorenzo...@gmail.com
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi all,
>>>>>
>>>>> To be more specific, when importing in warp you should use the pixel
>>>>> size of the images you gave to aretomo (which might be different from the
>>>>> "original pixel size" if your binning factor in WARP is not zero!).
>>>>>
>>>>> VolZ = 0 in AreTomo means "give me the aligned tilt series instead of
>>>>> a reconstruction". From the help:
>>>>> ```
>>>>> -VolZ
>>>>>    1. Volume z height for reconstrunction. It must be
>>>>>       greater than 0 to reconstruct a volume.
>>>>>    2. Default is 0, only aligned tilt series will
>>>>>       generated.
>>>>> ```
>>>>>
>>>>> On Thu, Jun 15, 2023 at 1:05 PM Felix Lange <felix.l...@gmail.com>
>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi all,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Not sure, if this applies to AreTomo reconstructions as well but in
>>>>>> Jesses' case the entry for pixel size should be the original pixel size of
>>>>>> the images (not the binned pixel size you used for AreTomo). You can also
>>>>>> export a deconvolved verison of the tomogram to have a better contrast.
>>>>>> Good luck!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Felix
>>>>>>
>>>>>> mrJ schrieb am Donnerstag, 15. Juni 2023 um 12:43:24 UTC+2:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi all,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> thank you for your responses.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> The AREtomo command I used is:
>>>>>>> (ARETOMO_executable_path) -InMrc TS_01.mrc -OutMrc
>>>>>>> TS_01_bin8_correcttiltangle.mrc -VolZ 1640 -OutBin 8 -AngFile
>>>>>>> TS_01_tiltangle.txt -Wbp 1 -Patch 4 4 -OutImod 2 -TiltCor -1
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> The binning in AREtomo is 8. When I reimport into WARP, for the
>>>>>>> "IMPORT TILT SERIES FROM IMOD" window I enter 17.464 for pixel size (binned
>>>>>>> 8X) and the dose is 4.4 per tilt (180 total dose, 41 tilts). Everything
>>>>>>> imports correctly. For AREtomo I used the Wbp 1 flag, so it's WBP I
>>>>>>> believe. I am not sure what WARP uses.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Not sure what you mean if I use VolZ 0... if the volume of Z for
>>>>>>> AREtomo is zero then there will be no tomogram generated. I'll try that.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Jesse
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> On Wednesday, June 14, 2023 at 5:34:34 PM UTC+2 lorenzo...@gmail.com
>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Hi Jesse,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> As mentioned by Tom, the only file needed (and read) by Warp is the
>>>>>>>> `xf` file. This gets combined with the angles from the mdoc file to get a
>>>>>>>> full set of transformations for tilt series alignment.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Some ideas:
>>>>>>>> - did you provide `-TiltCor` in AreTomo? If so, you need to update
>>>>>>>> the mdoc file to match the new angles. Otherwise, disable it entirely (-1)
>>>>>>>> or set it to 0.
>>>>>>>> - did you provide all the reconstruction-related options (dose,
>>>>>>>> pixel size, etc) and are you sure the units are correct?
>>>>>>>> - did you use Sart (default) or WBP for reconstruction? Sart looks
>>>>>>>> much more contrasty to the naked eye; try -Wbp to check whether that is the
>>>>>>>> difference you see.
>>>>>>>> - does the aligned tilt series look well aligned if you use -VolZ 0?
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Good luck!
>>>>>>>> Lorenzo
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Wed, Jun 14, 2023 at 5:30 PM Pranav Shah <
>>>>>>>> Prana...@strubi.ox.ac.uk> wrote:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> If you corrected for pre-tilt in aretomo youbwill get poor results.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> On Wed, 14 Jun 2023 at 16:03, tom dendooven <tom.de...@gmail.com>
>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Hi Jesse,
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Are you looking at deconvolved tomograms from warp or unfiltered
>>>>>>>>>> ones? Or is the binning significantly different between the Warp and
>>>>>>>>>> aretomo tomograms?
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Warp doesn't need a TAsolution.log file.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Best wishes,
>>>>>>>>>> Tom
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> On Wed, 14 Jun 2023 at 15:31, Jesse Hansen <bluu...@gmail.com>
>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> hi all,
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> I started in WARP for movie alignment then exported the stack
>>>>>>>>>>> "to IMOD".   However I used AREtomo to process instead of Etomo, using the
>>>>>>>>>>> flag -OUTImod 2 in order to get the xf file for importing into WARP.  The
>>>>>>>>>>> AREtomo reconstruction looks fantastic.   Now I reimport into WARP without
>>>>>>>>>>> issue, estimate the CTF, check handedness, then reconstruct tomograms.
>>>>>>>>>>> It all succeeds, but the output tomograms are really poor quality and I
>>>>>>>>>>> can't figure out why.   They certainly look similar to AREtomo in that I
>>>>>>>>>>> can see some proteins where they should be, but the contrast is really
>>>>>>>>>>> poor.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Inverting contrast in tomo reconstruction didn't help.   The FFT
>>>>>>>>>>> looks nice and has resolution to 6A, clear Thon rings which match well with
>>>>>>>>>>> the calculated CTF.  AREtomo did not output a TAsolutions.log file that I
>>>>>>>>>>> Can find, which I thought WARP needs.  However WARP did seem to
>>>>>>>>>>> successfully import the data using just the XF file.   I tried clicking
>>>>>>>>>>> "dont invert tilt angles" during import because perhaps AREtomo is
>>>>>>>>>>> different from IMOD (explained in WARP tutorial) but that had no effect.
>>>>>>>>>>> Hard to tell which was better.  I tried reprocessing AREtomo without the
>>>>>>>>>>> flag -FlipVol hoping that might fix an issue with the coordinate system but
>>>>>>>>>>> it also had no effect.  Overall it seems like the protein is definitely
>>>>>>>>>>> visible in the final reconstruction but it just looks really poor
>>>>>>>>>>> contrast.  Maybe something is going wrong with the CTF correction or...
>>>>>>>>>>> maybe it's just that the WARP reconstruction looks WAY worse than the
>>>>>>>>>>> AREtomo reconstruction.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Any help appreciated, thanks.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Jesse
>>>>>>>>>>>
